2606.07113.title: Beyond Post-hoc Explanation: Toward Glassbox AI via Probabilistic Mediation
2606.07113.paper_summary: Large language models are rapidly becoming infrastructural components in high-stakes institutional settings, including public administration, legal reasoning, and healthcare, where opacity is not merely inconvenient but institutionally and legally untenable. Existing approaches to explainability are predominantly post-hoc, offering unstable, non-contestable accounts that have no formal relationship to the reasoning process that produced the output. We argue that the problem is not the absence of explanation but the absence of structured reasoning in the first place. This paper makes the case for a fundamentally different architecture, which we call the Glassbox Framework, in which Bayesian networks serve as transparent, ante-hoc mediation layers for generative models. Bayesian networks encode domain knowledge, causal assumptions, and probabilistic dependencies before inference occurs, enabling auditable reasoning traces, uncertainty quantification, and contestable outputs. We characterise the architecture of this framework and ground it in a benefit eligibility scenario, identifying the foundational challenges spanning semantic alignment, dynamic model construction, probabilistic grounding, and human governance that must be solved to realise it at scale. By shifting from post-hoc explanation to ante-hoc probabilistic mediation, this work outlines a principled path toward AI systems that are not only powerful but fundamentally accountable.
2606.07113.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型正迅速成為高風險機構環境中的基礎設施組件，包括公共行政、法律推理和醫療保健，在這些領域中，不透明性不僅是個不便，而是機構上和法律上無法接受的。現有的可解釋性方法主要是事後解釋，提供不穩定、不可爭辯的說明，與產生輸出的推理過程沒有正式的關係。我們認為問題不在於缺乏解釋，而在於根本缺乏結構化的推理。本文主張一種根本不同的架構，我們稱之為玻璃盒框架，其中貝葉斯網絡作為生成模型的透明、事前中介層。貝葉斯網絡在推理發生之前編碼領域知識、因果假設和概率依賴性，從而實現可審計的推理痕跡、不確定性量化和可爭辯的輸出。我們描述了這一框架的架構，並將其根植於一個福利資格場景中，識別出必須解決的基礎挑戰，包括語義對齊、動態模型構建、概率基礎和人類治理，以便在大規模上實現它。通過從事後解釋轉向事前概率中介，這項工作勾勒出一條原則性的道路，朝著不僅強大而且根本負責的人工智慧系統邁進。
2606.07113.author: Manuele Leonelli et.al.
